phase-scope.phase-39: Close the remaining issued Telegram proxy unavailable state with redacted local/static and operator-live evidence.
phase-scope.supersedes: M-050 remains the edge-routing module; M-051 adds the missing availability proof layer above routing and policy health.

commands.command-4: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py tests/test_mtproto_de_edge_contract.py tests/test_deploy_phase38_static.py tests/test_mtproto_availability_diagnostics.py -v
commands.command-6: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-051.xml docs/plans/Phase-39.xml docs/verification/V-M-051.xml && mygrace lint

verification-levels.module-local: Static checks prove link format, redaction, fallback-port contracts, and diagnostic schema without live secrets.
verification-levels.wave-level: M-043, M-044, M-045, M-050, and M-012 run together because issued-link payload, runtime policy, UI connection action, DE edge, and deploy guard form one availability behavior.
verification-levels.phase-level: Operator-live smoke proves one active assignment through public RU 443, DE fake-TLS accept, Telegram downstream reachability, and Telegram Desktop available status.

module-checks.check-1: Backend and runtime secret derivation parity remains stable for issued SNI without printing the derived secret.
module-checks.check-2: Generated owner link supports the primary Telegram connection action and full-link copy flow.
module-checks.check-3: DE runtime fallback guard rejects stale 127.0.0.1:8443 for Phase-39 and expects private 9443 fallback.
module-checks.check-4: Phase-39 diagnostics redact tg links, derived secrets, base secret, salt, runtime token, private keys, JWTs, and raw .env content.
module-checks.check-5: One active assignment is present, active, policy-applied, and counted in DE policy health.
module-checks.check-6: Fake-TLS probe receives a response through public RU 443 to DE runtime using the issued assignment derivation.
module-checks.check-7: DE runtime can reach at least one Telegram downstream target or emits a separate downstream failure code.
module-checks.check-8: Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available using the refreshed primary connection action.

verification-matrix.gate-1: Issued assignment payload, SNI, port, and link assembly remain stable and do not rotate existing user credentials.
verification-matrix.gate-2: Runtime policy diagnostics distinguish policy-applied, policy-missing, bridge-unreachable, and adapter-rejected states without leaking credentials.
verification-matrix.gate-3: User cabinet exposes a primary Telegram connection action and full-link copy while keeping manual fields secondary and mobile-safe.
verification-matrix.gate-4: Deploy/static smoke rejects stale DE fallback 8443 and preserves the Phase-38 private 9443 web fallback versus public admin 8443 split.
verification-matrix.gate-5: Public RU 443 routes an issued MTProto SNI to DE and the fake-TLS probe receives a runtime response.
verification-matrix.gate-6: Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available after using the refreshed primary connection action.

scenarios.scenario-1: Verified user opens cabinet, clicks the primary Telegram action, and Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available.
scenarios.scenario-2: Operator runs redacted live smoke and receives assignment, policy, route, fake-TLS, and downstream success without secrets.
scenarios.scenario-3: Policy health is OK but fake-TLS accept fails; diagnostics classify secret/link mismatch instead of route failure.
scenarios.scenario-4: Fake-TLS accept succeeds but downstream Telegram connection fails; diagnostics classify Telegram DC/provider reachability separately.
scenarios.scenario-5: Stale DE fallback 8443 is detected and blocked from being treated as Phase-39-ready.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Never print full tg://proxy links, https://t.me/proxy links, derived secret, base secret, salt, runtime token, wildcard private key, JWTs, refresh tokens, Resend key, or raw .env values.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Live evidence may include assignment id, user id, masked SNI, status codes, policy count, safe hash fingerprints, route role names, and safe failure codes only.

telemetry-requirements.metric-1: phase39_assignment_probe_status tagged found, missing, inactive, duplicate, or query_failed.
telemetry-requirements.metric-2: phase39_policy_status tagged healthy, degraded, unauthorized, unreachable, or policy_missing.
telemetry-requirements.metric-3: phase39_route_status tagged web_route, mtproto_route, unknown_route, connect_failed, or timeout.
telemetry-requirements.metric-4: phase39_fake_tls_status tagged accepted, invalid_digest, no_sni, no_response, or runtime_unreachable.
telemetry-requirements.metric-5: phase39_downstream_status tagged reachable, unreachable, bootstrap_empty, or provider_blocked.
telemetry-requirements.metric-6: phase39_desktop_proof_status tagged operator_available, operator_unavailable, not_run, or stale_client_state.

live-smoke-gates.gate-1: RU backend can identify exactly one active assignment for the selected test user.
live-smoke-gates.gate-2: DE policy health succeeds privately and policy_count is non-zero.
live-smoke-gates.gate-3: RU HAProxy routes issued SNI traffic to the DE backend.
live-smoke-gates.gate-4: Fake-TLS probe receives a server response through public RU 443.
live-smoke-gates.gate-5: DE reaches at least one Telegram downstream target.
live-smoke-gates.gate-6: Operator Telegram Desktop proof reports available.

failure-handoff.item-1: If route fails, inspect RU HAProxy SNI match and wildcard DNS before changing secrets.
failure-handoff.item-2: If fake-TLS accept fails, compare redacted derivation fingerprints and user link source before rotating anything.
failure-handoff.item-3: If downstream fails, keep user link stable and investigate DE provider/Telegram DC reachability separately.
failure-handoff.item-4: If Telegram Desktop alone fails after fake-TLS probe passes, clear old proxy entries and retry with the refreshed primary Telegram link before code changes.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, static smoke, backend fake/private adapter tests, frontend build, redaction checks, and deterministic link-format checks.
autonomous-execution-safety.safe-server-readonly: Read-only server diagnostics that do not print .env values, tokens, full links, private keys, or secrets.
autonomous-execution-safety.requires-operator: Applying live deploy changes, restarting RU/DE services, and accepting Telegram Desktop proof as final availability evidence.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-action: Secret rotation, SNI reissue, policy token replacement, wildcard certificate replacement, and public firewall changes are out of scope unless a new explicit fix phase approves them.
